page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, containing 48 physical cores and supporting 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-threaded operations and cryptographic tasks. The server achieves top 10% placement in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Passmark database operations. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference prompt processing for small and medium models, alongside top-tier static web serving throughput. However, single-core stress-ng performance and text generation for large LLM models fall into the average category, highlighting a performance profile optimized for parallelized, compute-heavy execution rather than single-threaded speed.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highcpu-96 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high compute capacity without the overhead of large memory footprints or bundled local storage. The absence of local NVMe drives and GPUs reduces unnecessary hardware costs for pure CPU-bound tasks. Tradeoffs include the low 2 GB RAM per vCPU ratio, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases, and the lack of complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This makes it highly efficient for parallel processing, software compilation, and web serving.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server configured with 96 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 physical cores and a 2.3 GHz clock speed, this x86_64 instance features a 260 MB L3 cache and dedicated CPU allocation. It contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in multi-core processing, database operations, static web serving, and LLM prompt processing, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, CPU-bound workloads by avoiding the expense of unused memory or storage. It is designed for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It delivers top-tier multi-core and database performance without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing and high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier multi-core, database, and static web serving benchmark performance
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimizing cost for pure compute tasks
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and parallel processing
